name: Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) (République Démocratique du Congo)
summary: The Democratic Republic of the Congo is a Central African civil-law unitary republic whose family-law framework operates under the Family Code 1987 (Code de la Famille, Law 87-010 of 1 August 1987, as amended by Law 16/008 of 2016) drawing on Belgian civil-law substantive heritage (via colonial inheritance) with codification of customary-law marriage provisions. Parental authority and child custody are governed by Family Code arts. 317-340. The Court of Cassation (Cour de Cassation) is the apex court for civil and criminal matters; the Constitutional Court (Cour Constitutionnelle) operates separate constitutional review. Family-law matters are heard at first instance in the Peace Tribunal (Tribunal de Paix) for matters of competence and the Court of First Instance (Tribunal de Grande Instance) for higher-value matters. Psychology profession is regulated through the Ministry of Health framework with the National Order of Psychologists. DRC is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label; courts operate substantively under the interest-of-the-child standard. DRC is non-Hague Convention.

statutory_framework.citation: Family Code 1987 (Law 87-010, as amended by Law 16/008 of 2016) arts. 317-340
statutory_framework.title: Family Code — Parental authority and custody
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Family Code drawing on Belgian civil-law substantive heritage with codification of customary-law marriage provisions. Substantially amended by Law 16/008 of 2016. Arts. 317-340 govern parental authority and child custody.

statutory_framework.title: Law on Child Protection
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Law on Child Protection aligned with UNCRC obligations.

apex_courts.name: Court of Cassation (Cour de Cassation)
apex_courts.role: Apex court for civil and criminal matters.

apex_courts.name: Constitutional Court (Cour Constitutionnelle)
apex_courts.role: Constitutional Court with original jurisdiction over constitutional review.

professional_regulators.name: National Order of Psychologists of the DRC
professional_regulators.role: National Order of Psychologists under Ministry of Health framework.

anonymisation_convention: DRC family-court decisions are anonymised per Court of Cassation practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Congo independence + Lumumba + Katanga-crisis framework
key_developments.description: Congo independence 30 June 1960 from Belgium — Patrice Lumumba first prime minister. Subsequent Katanga secession crisis 1960-1963 substantively distinctive Central African post-colonial crisis framework. Lumumba assassination 17 January 1961 substantively affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Mobutu coup + Zaire + 32-year-authoritarian framework
key_developments.description: Mobutu Sese Seko coup d'état 24 November 1965 — substantively distinctive African 32-year-authoritarian framework 1965-1997. Zaire renamed 1971. Substantively distinctive Central African Cold-War-era-personalist-authoritarianism framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Family Code 1987 (Law 87-010)
key_developments.description: Federal Family Code enacted 1 August 1987 drawing on Belgian civil-law substantive heritage with codification of customary-law marriage provisions. Arts. 317-340 govern parental authority and child custody. Substantively significant family-law-codification framework persisting through subsequent regime-changes.

key_developments.title: DRC ratifies UN Convention on the Rights of the Child
key_developments.description: DRC (then Zaire) ratified the UNCRC on 27 September 1990 — substantively significant child-rights framework integration. Foundational best-interests-of-the-child framework integration with subsequent 2009 Law on Child Protection substantive doctrine.

key_developments.title: First Congo War 1996-1997 + Laurent-Désiré Kabila + Mobutu-fall framework
key_developments.description: First Congo War 24 October 1996 – 16 May 1997 ending Mobutu era. Laurent-Désiré Kabila assumed presidency 17 May 1997 — Zaire renamed Democratic Republic of the Congo. Substantive 21st-century African armed-conflict framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Second Congo War 1998-2003 + Africa's-World-War + most-devastating-conflict framework
key_developments.description: Second Congo War 2 August 1998 – 18 July 2003 — substantively distinctive globally 'Africa's World War' framework involving 9 African countries + 25 armed groups. Estimated 5.4 million killed (largest conflict-death toll since WWII). Substantive demographic and family-law-implementation disruption affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Constitution of DRC 2006 + post-war democratic-transition framework
key_developments.description: Constitution of Democratic Republic of the Congo 2006 adopted 18 February 2006 by referendum substantively reforming political-institutional framework establishing post-war democratic-transition framework. Substantive constitutional anchor for contemporary family-law jurisprudence.

key_developments.title: Law on Child Protection (Law 09/001) + Kivu-armed-conflict context
key_developments.description: Federal Law on Child Protection enacted Law 09/001 aligned with UNCRC obligations. Substantive ongoing Kivu armed-conflict framework substantially affecting child-protection-implementation. Substantive child-protection-framework consolidation within Belgian-derivative Central African framework.

key_developments.title: Family Code amendments (Law 16/008) + child-marriage-and-monogamy-reform framework
key_developments.description: Substantive amendments to Family Code provisions Law 16/008 of 15 July 2016 — substantive child-marriage-prohibition (age 18 minimum) + monogamy-default + gender-equality codification. Substantively significant Belgian-derivative Central African family-law-modernisation framework.

key_developments.title: Tshisekedi re-election + M23 conflict continuation + CNDH framework + UN-MONUSCO withdrawal
key_developments.description: Félix Tshisekedi re-elected 20 December 2023 substantively significant continuing-presidency framework. M23 armed-conflict continuation in eastern DRC 2023 substantively affecting demographic and family-law adjudication including IDP-family cases. Commission Nationale des Droits de l'Homme (CNDH) institutional position addressing PA-related issues within Family Code + Law 16/008 framework. UN MONUSCO withdrawal trajectory 2023-2025 substantively significant institutional context. Substantively significant Central African institutional jurisprudential development.

key_developments.title: Cour de Cassation + Cour Constitutionnelle — interest-of-the-child substantive register + Tshisekedi + M23 context
key_developments.description: Cour de Cassation and Cour Constitutionnelle continue to develop interest-of-the-child jurisprudence under Family Code 1987 + Law 16/008 + Law on Child Protection 2009 framework in custody disputes within Félix Tshisekedi presidency framework (since 2019). Ongoing M23 armed-conflict in eastern DRC substantively affecting family-law-implementation. Substantive analysis without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label adoption.

structural_findings: DRC operates a Belgian-civil-law family-law framework with codification of customary-law marriage provisions — places DRC in the Central African Francophone civil-law cluster with structurally distinctive Belgian-derivative substantive heritage (vs French-derivative Central African states).
Belgian-derivative civil-law tradition is structurally distinctive within Central Africa — DRC shares this pattern with Rwanda and Burundi within the corpus.
Non-Hague Convention status places DRC in the non-Hague Central African cluster.
editorial_notes: DRC jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-09 from 3 to 10 key_developments with full Lumumba-to-Tshisekedi trajectory: 1960-Congo-independence-+-Lumumba-+-Katanga-crisis + 1965-Mobutu-coup-+-Zaire-+-32-year-authoritarian + 1987-Family-Code-Law-87-010 + 1990-UNCRC-ratification + 1996-First-Congo-War-+-Laurent-Désiré-Kabila + 1998-Second-Congo-War-+-Africa's-World-War + 2006-Constitution-of-DRC-+-post-war-democratic-transition + 2009-Law-on-Child-Protection-Law-09-001 + 2016-Family-Code-amendments-Law-16-008-+-child-marriage-and-monogamy-reform + 2024-Cour-de-Cassation-+-Cour-Constitutionnelle-+-Tshisekedi-+-M23.
Civil-law Central African Francophone (Belgian-derivative + Family Code 1987 + 2016 amendments + Law on Child Protection 2009 + non-Hague Convention).

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — substantive interest-of-the-child analysis under Family Code 1987 + Law 16/008 + Law on Child Protection 2009 framework without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label.

Joins Central-African + Francophone + civil-law + Belgian-derivative (with Rwanda, Burundi) + Lumumba-Patrice-1960-1961 + Mobutu-Sese-Seko-32-year-authoritarian-Zaire-1965-1997 + First-Congo-War-1996-1997 + Second-Congo-War-1998-2003-Africa's-World-War-9-countries-25-armed-groups-5.4-million-killed-largest-conflict-death-toll-since-WWII-distinctive-globally + M23-armed-conflict-eastern-DRC-context + Tshisekedi-presidency + non-Hague-Convention clusters within the corpus.
